response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack and web engineer with 15+ years across web, mobile, and some embedded work, but the profile is a poor match for this role. The position is centered on embedded LAN-switch firmware, strong C for UNIX development, Linux kernel source modification, and networking/security domains (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3, NETCONF/SNMP) — none of which appear in the resume beyond a brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments. Most critically, the role requires native-level Japanese, and the candidate lists only English, Romanian, and basic German.
strengths: Deep, broad software engineering experience (15+ years) with strong testing/TDD discipline
Some low-level/embedded and C/C++ exposure (National Instruments LabWindows/CVI, robotic arms, audio synthesis)
Proven ownership of full development lifecycle and cross-functional delivery
concerns: No native-level Japanese — a hard requirement for this role
No demonstrated security (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption) or switch/networking (L2/L3, NETCONF, SNMP) experience
No Linux kernel analysis/source-modification or chip-vendor SDK / embedded firmware background; C is not a core strength

key_factors.factor: Limited embedded C / Linux kernel depth
